tickets.title: US001 - Persona: Student
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to scan a textbook's barcode using my phone camera, so that its ISBN and Title are automatically filled without typing.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Verify that opening the scanner successfully activates the device's camera.
- Verify that scanning a valid textbook barcode auto-populates the Title and ISBN fields.
- Verify that an error message is displayed if the barcode is unreadable or not found in the database.

tickets.title: US002 - Persona: Student
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to list my items for auction instead of a fixed price, so that I can sell them to the highest bidder before the semester ends.
Acceptance Criteria:
- Verify that sellers can select "Auction" as a listing type and set a starting price and end date.
- Verify that buyers can place a bid higher than the current highest bid.
- Verify that the system automatically closes the auction at the specified end date and notifies the winner.

tickets.title: US003 - Persona: Student
tickets.description:
As a Student, I want to select an official Safe Exchange Zone before starting a chat with a seller, so that I can ensure my physical safety during meetups.

Acceptance Criteria:
- Verify that the app forces the user to select a designated Safe Exchange Zone (e.g., library lobby) before opening the chat interface.
- Verify that the selected safe zone location is displayed clearly inside the chat window for both users.

analysis.ambiguity1: The 2% Transaction Fee: The Legal Team says the app cannot process payments (cash/Venmo only), so it is unclear how the app can track or collect a 2% fee.
analysis.ambiguity2: Barcode Scanning: The user wants to scan barcodes for book details, but it is vague where the book data (Title/Author) will be fetched from (e.g., an external API or bookstore database).
analysis.conflictSolution:
The Conflict: The Student Union wants a 2% transaction fee, but the Legal Team forbids the app from handling any money.
Proposed Solution: Remove the transaction fee. Instead, charge a flat "Listing Fee" via an official university payment portal, or use a "Freemium" model where student clubs pay to pin ads on the app.
analysis.nfr1: Security (Access Control): Restrict registration exclusively to valid ".edu" email addresses.
analysis.nfr2: Safety (Operational Constraint): Force users to select a "Safe Exchange Zone" before opening the chat.
analysis.nfr3: Usability: Integrate camera hardware for quick, automatic barcode scanning.
